response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems software engineer with solid low-latency and distributed systems experience, but his background has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, SIMD/MIMD architectures, GPU workload analysis, and deep-learning accelerator optimization — none of which appear anywhere in the resume. His AI-related work is limited to software-layer agentic tooling, not hardware or ML performance engineering. He meets the educational floor (BS + 14+ years) but the domain mismatch is fundamental and cannot be bridged by adjacent skills.
strengths: 14+ years of software engineering across diverse stacks, including low-latency systems handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic
Demonstrated performance optimization mindset: async I/O, caching, fiber-based concurrency, and high-throughput database work in DLang
Breadth across embedded, cloud infrastructure, and developer tooling shows adaptability across system layers
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the core technical requirement of the role
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator background whatsoever
No deep-learning workload analysis or hardware-software co-design experience; AI exposure is limited to software-layer agent frameworks
